channel.title: Welcome! on Theo's Park

item.title: Hello World
item.description: I just made a website yay! Hugo is a life saver.
item.content: I just made a website yay! Hugo is a life saver.

item.title: About Me
item.description: Hi! My name is Theo, and I do… a lot of different things. Academics I am a student at Purdue University studying computer science and philosophy. When I was in middle school, I would collect ISO files for old operating systems, (shout out to WinWorld), install them on virtual machines, and be fascinated by not only the OS themselves but the installation processes. While I did not have a coding background besides a little bit of C from CS50, I knew I loved playing with computers.
item.content:
Hi! My name is Theo, and I do… a lot of different things.

Academics

I am a student at Purdue University studying computer science and philosophy. When I was in middle school, I would collect ISO files for old operating systems, (shout out to WinWorld), install them on virtual machines, and be fascinated by not only the OS themselves but the installation processes. While I did not have a coding background besides a little bit of C from CS50, I knew I loved playing with computers. This turns out to be true, and I enjoy studying computer science. My favorite courses include CS18000: OOP, CS182: Discrete math for CS, CS240: C Programming, but my all time favorite is MA351: Linear algebra with Professor Sinclair.

I also like writing and math, particularly logics and discrete math, which helped me majoring in philosophy along with the influence from my dad.

Outside of the school work, I am a member of Undergraduate Student Board for the CS department.

Career

My love for teaching is reflected on some of my past and current jobs. I worked as a undergraduate teaching assistant for CS18000: Problem Solving and Objected-Oriented Programming since the second semester of the freshman year to the following summer. I also worked as a course content developer during the same summer, developing homework, projects, and exams for CS180 using JUnit framework.

In the fall semester of the sophomore year, I will be working as a lecturer for CS193000: Tools, teaching 800+ computer science and data science freshmen CS tools such as Git, *nix shell and commands, and Vim.

You can view my resume for the detailed explanations.

More about me

I love messing with shell, particularly different text editors, but I always come back to Vim and Neovim. Check out my dotfiles for the vim/Nvim configuration.

I love taking photos. I own Canon EOS M50 with EF-M 15-45mm f/3.5-6.3 lens, and I have taken many portraits with it. Maybe one day I will share my photos online.

I am colorblind.

I love writing, despite being not particularly good at it.

I love organization and cleaning.

I love salmon.

item.title: My Projects
item.description: Hi! You can find descriptions and links to my projects here!
item.content: Hi! You can find descriptions and links to my projects here!
